name: Keramas / Pantai Keramas
gps_location: https://maps.app.goo.gl/?q=-8.5897,115.3322 (approx. -8.5897, 115.3322, Keramas Beach, Blahbatuh, Gianyar Regency, Bali; in front of Komune Resort) [uncertain]
distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 35-40 km (about 45-60 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~60-75 min by scooter (~30 km via bypass); From Uluwatu: ~75-90 min by scooter (~45-50 km) [uncertain]
wave_type: Right-hand reef break (rivermouth-influenced) over shallow volcanic rock/reef; fast, hollow, world-class performance wave with steep take-off into a near-vertical bowl, then long fat shoulder with end-section ramps for aerials
skill_level: Advanced to Pro (on smaller days surfable by confident regular-foot intermediates; goofy-footers find it backhand and harder); not suitable for beginners
wave_height_range: Surfable 2-10 ft; sweet spot 4-6 ft (chest to double-overhead); turns on and barrels once it's overhead
bottom_type: Shallow volcanic rock reef with rivermouth sand influence (sand on inside, sharp volcanic rock on the main reef)
best_season: Wet season (November-April) is prime; transition months March-April and October-November are also excellent with favorable winds; surfable year-round but smaller and less consistent in dry season
best_tide: Mid to high tide preferred (high tide for safer reef coverage); low tide exposes sharp volcanic reef and gets dangerous
best_wind: Offshore NW to N wind (wet-season pattern); light morning offshore winds are typical of the East Coast in the wet season
best_swell_direction: S to SSW (180-220 degrees); more south in the swell means bigger Keramas; SE/E swells also work
swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 in wet season (most consistent East Coast performance wave); 2-3/5 in dry season (smaller, less reliable)
wave_length_meters: approx. 80-150 m on a good set (steep take-off bowl + long shoulder + end-section)
wave_speed_rating: fast (high-performance speed wave; transitions from deep to shallow water very quickly making it hollow and demanding)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: High - steep, near-vertical take-off bowl that hollows out immediately; shallow volcanic reef beneath; punishing on late drops
secondary_peaks: Main right (the famous bowl + shoulder); end-section ramp/aerial closeout; small inside reform on sand near the rivermouth on bigger days; no real left option
peak_crowd_level: 4-5/5 in wet season firing days (huge crowds; one of Bali's most crowded performance waves alongside Uluwatu); 2-3/5 on average days; less crowded at dawn and during paid night sessions
access: Direct beach access via Komune Beach Club Bali / Keramas Beach Road off the Bali-Padang Bay coastal road in Blahbatuh, Gianyar. Park at Komune Resort or roadside; short walk down to black-sand beach. About 45-60 min from DPS airport by car
paddle_out_method: Straightforward beach paddle-out directly from the black sand in front of Komune Resort; paddle out the channel on the south/inside of the peak; time sets to avoid getting washed onto reef
entry_fee: No formal entry fee for the beach; parking ~IDR 5,000-10,000 (scooter) / ~IDR 10,000-20,000 (car); Komune Beach Club has paid day-passes (~IDR 250,000-500,000) with pool, lockers, hot showers [uncertain]
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (sunrise to ~8 am) before the trade winds pick up and crowds arrive; also paid night sessions under Komune floodlights
best_time_of_day: Sunrise to ~8 am for cleanest conditions and lighter crowds; mid-morning gets very crowded; evening night-surf sessions (booked, max ~6 surfers) at Komune floodlights
nearby_warung: Komune Beach Club restaurants and beach bar, Swan Restaurant Keramas, Romano Italian Restaurant & Pizzeria, Pizza Napoli, local warungs (Lawar Plex WMD) along Jalan Pantai Keramas; Sababay Winery nearby
alternative_spot_within_10min: Within ~10-20 min drive: Lebih Beach (~5-10 min west, sand-bottom rivermouth), Pabean (small reef break), Padang Galak (~20 min west); Ketewel reef further north on bigger swells
hazards: Shallow sharp volcanic rock reef (breaks bones, deep cuts), strong rivermouth currents, hold-downs in the bowl, crowded lineup with aggressive locals, occasional submerged debris from the river, hot black sand on the beach, motorbike traffic on coastal road
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the reef, occasional jellyfish (especially after rain), rare sea snake sightings, no significant shark issue; treat reef cuts immediately with fresh water flush, antiseptic, and clinic visit if deep [uncertain]
reef_cut_severity_index: Very high - sharp volcanic rock reef; some of Bali's most frequent reef-cut injuries; reef boots advisable on low-tide sessions, helmet considered by some pros
rip_current_pattern: Strong rivermouth rip on the south/inside of the peak (often used as paddle channel); cross-shore sweep during bigger SE/S swells; outgoing tide intensifies pull toward the reef [uncertain]
lineup_etiquette: Strict hierarchy - locals and Komune crew have priority; do not drop in, do not paddle inside on take-off, wait your turn, no shouting; smile and acknowledge regulars; dawn patrol earns more respect than midday show-up
localism_intensity: High (cited 7-8/10) - reputed as worse than Uluwatu or Bingin; the resident 'ripper crew' and Komune-sponsored surfers dominate; drop-ins can lead to aggressive confrontation
respect_protocol: Sit wider and deeper than you think; do not paddle around for sets; let locals have priority; no flash photography or drones over heads without permission; tip warungs and parking attendants
resident_local_legends: Raditya Rondi, Garut Widiarta, Bronson Meidy, Mega Semadhi (visits) and the Komune-affiliated local crew are known Keramas regulars; many Indonesian national team riders train here [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Active Hindu coastal village - respect ceremonial processions on the beach and Pura Segara (sea temple) areas; observe Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
medical_nearby: Komune Resort has on-site first aid; nearby clinics: Klinik Pratama Keramas, Klinik Utama Permata Hati Gianyar; for serious injuries Sanglah/Prof. Ngoerah General Hospital Denpasar (~30 km), BIMC Hospital Kuta (~35 km), Siloam Hospital Bali
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Klinik Pratama Keramas/Blahbatuh approx. 3-5 km; RSUD Sanjiwani Gianyar approx. 10-12 km; BIMC Kuta approx. 35 km [uncertain]
rescue_jet_ski_available: Komune Resort has provided jet-ski water safety during WSL events and major contests; not on permanent daily standby for the public [uncertain]
emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Bali Search and Rescue (BASARNAS) 115; Komune Resort front desk +62 361 301 8888; RSUD Sanjiwani Gianyar +62 361 943-049 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: Komune Surf Academy (in-house at Komune Resort); Keramas Surf Camp; lessons typically run on the gentler inside / mellow days only - main peak is not a teaching wave
board_rental_availability: Yes - Komune Resort board hire, Keramas Surf Camp, and several local rentals along Jalan Pantai Keramas; quality shortboards approx. USD 15/day (IDR ~220,000-250,000)
surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land photographers shoot from the beach daily; water photographers in lineup on bigger days; Komune-affiliated shooters and freelancers
photographer_recommendation: Komune-affiliated photographer packages approx. USD 65/day; freelance options on Instagram (search 'keramas surf photographer'); session prices typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Hotel Komune Resort & Beach Club Bali (oceanfront luxury, on the wave), Keramas Surf Camp (mid-range with surf packages), Keramas Surf Hostel (budget dorms), Blue Coco Guesthouse, Keramas Moonlight Villa, Keramas Sacred River Retreat
webcam_url: Surfline Keramas cam (premium) https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/keramas/ ; Komune Resort live cam on their website [uncertain]
drone_allowed: Generally allowed from the beach for personal use; do not fly over the lineup during WSL events or peak sessions, and respect Komune Resort guests' privacy; standard Indonesian drone rules apply [uncertain]
coworking_nearby: No coworking directly at Keramas; Ubud-area coworking (Outpost Ubud, Hubud) ~30-40 min drive; Sanur coworking ~30 min south [uncertain]
avg_lesson_price: Group surf lesson approx. IDR 500,000-800,000 (USD 35-55) at Komune Surf Academy / Keramas Surf Camp; private lessons IDR 800,000-1,500,000 [uncertain]
board_rental_daily: Approx. IDR 150,000-250,000 (USD 10-17) per day for shortboard at local rentals; Komune Resort and surf camps higher end USD 15-25/day
water_quality_rating: Generally fair to good in dry season; can drop in wet season due to rivermouth runoff carrying soil, palm fronds and occasional plastics from upstream after heavy rain [uncertain]
annual_competition_window: WSL Championship Tour 'Corona Bali Pro' / Corona Open J-Bay equivalent has been hosted at Keramas (notably 2018, 2019, 2023, 2024); typically scheduled in the May-June window when CT visits Indonesia; also various Indonesian national and longboard events through the year [uncertain]
community_events: Komune Beach Club hosts regular surf and DJ events, Friday/Saturday night parties, and surf film screenings; occasional youth surf clinics with Indonesian national team riders [uncertain]
